channel.title: physics.gen-ph updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: physics.gen-ph updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: Spin-induced and orbital quantum backflow in Pauli theory
item.description: arXiv:2606.15806v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We show that quantum backflow in spin-$\tfrac{1}{2}$ systems within the Pauli framework can arise from spin-dependent contributions to the probability current. We construct explicit two-mode states in which the orbital current reduces to a uniform drift, so that no interference between longitudinal momentum components is present in this sector. In such configurations, backflow is generated entirely by spinor structure, which induces time-dependent modulations of the local current leading to flux reversal, while the longitudinal momentum distribution remains strictly supported on positive momenta. We derive analytical conditions for backflow in terms of spinor and momentum parameters, allowing direct control of the effect via internal degrees of freedom. We further analyze spatial coarse-graining, showing exponential suppression of interference and a qualitatively different scaling of orbital and spin contributions, with spin-induced backflow remaining robust in regimes where orbital effects are strongly suppressed.
item.dc:creator: Tomasz Rado\.zycki

item.title: First-Principles-Based Grand Unified Theory (GUT) for Micro-Macro Modal Quantization (MQ)--Part IV: Feynman Path Integral Formulation, Act 1
item.description: arXiv:1804.09246v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: In microscopic world, there exist many quantization phenomena, and scientists established some formally different (but essentially equivalent) quantization schemes, such as Heisenberg picture, Schrodinger picture, Dirac picture, and Feynman path integral formulation. In macroscopic/classical electrodynamics, there also exists so-called macroscopic modal quantization (MMQ) phenomenon. In our previous papers, the Heisenberg, Schrodinger, and Dirac pictures were generalized from microscopic modal quantization problem to MMQ problem. As a continuation of our previous works, this paper focuses on further generalizing the Feynman path integral formulation from microscopic modal quantization problem to MMQ problem.
item.dc:creator: Ren-Zun Lian
